summary: Closed, unmerged PR attempting to decompile two gmregclear functions, fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C, with header prototype updates and a local struct field recovery at lbl_80472D28.x10A. Evidence includes claimed and bot-reported 100% fuzzy matches, plus author comments explaining retained pointer math in fn_8017F14C and later saying the work was consolidated into other PRs.

key_files.role: Implemented fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C, split lbl_80472D28 padding to expose x10A, and retained pointer math for an unknown void* argument at offset 0x98.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmregclear.c hunk around struct lbl_80472D28_t

key_files.role: Updated prototypes for recovered function bodies from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmregclear.h fn_8017DE54 prototype changed to s32 fn_8017DE54(u8, u8*)
raw_diff: gmregclear.h fn_8017F14C prototype changed to s32 fn_8017F14C(void* arg0)

what_changed.text: Decompiled fn_8017DE54 as an s32-returning helper taking a u8 flag byte and u8* buffer, with flag checks for 0x20, 0x10, and 2 and comparisons against byte value 0x21.
Decompiled fn_8017F14C as an s32-returning helper taking void*, gated by gm_8016AE38()->x24C8.x4_7, clamping a value read from arg0+0x98 to 9999, and multiplying by lbl_80472D28.x10A.
Recovered a field in struct lbl_80472D28_t at offset 0x10A as s16 x10A by splitting prior padding.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct lbl_80472D28_t pad_108 changed and x10A added

what_changed.text: Updated public header declarations for fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C to concrete signatures instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

smart_moves.text: Kept header prototypes aligned with the newly recovered function bodies rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

Documented why the arg0+0x98 access in fn_8017F14C remained pointer math: the parameter was void*, no known decompiled callers were identified, and the closest candidate struct had unnamed padding at that offset.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function body proves a signature, update the owning header prototype in the same patch rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmregclear.h fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C prototype changes

decomp_lessons.lesson: Pointer math may remain as bridge code when the argument type is genuinely unknown and no field-bearing struct is supported by available caller/type evidence, but the reason should be recorded.

naming_conventions.name: x10A
naming_conventions.context: New s16 field recovered in lbl_80472D28_t at offset 0x10A; address-style/offset-style name preserves uncertainty rather than inventing semantics.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct lbl_80472D28_t adds s16 x10A

naming_conventions.name: fn_8017DE54 / fn_8017F14C
naming_conventions.context: Function names remained address-style despite body recovery; no semantic rename evidence was present in this PR slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: function definitions and header prototypes

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Clamp expression written as explicit if: val copied to ret, capped at 9999, then multiplied by recovered s16 field x10A.
Byte-buffer checks in fn_8017DE54 use explicit pointer p = &arg1[1] and compare three bytes to 0x21 before returning 3 - count.
Recovered a typed field from padding for lbl_80472D28.x10A instead of reading that global structure member by raw offset.

review_feedback.text: Author initially said pointer math would be fixed soon.

Author later stated the fn_8017F14C pointer math could not be replaced with struct field access because arg0 was void*, had no known callers in decomped code, and offset 0x98 was padding in the closest candidate struct.
Author said the work was consolidated into other PRs.

follow_up_queries.reason: Find whether later consolidated PRs recovered the real argument type or field for the retained pointer math.

follow_up_queries.query: gmregclear fn_8017DE54 fn_8017F14C consolidated into other PRs
follow_up_queries.reason: Locate the PR that absorbed this closed unmerged work and compare final accepted source shape.
Check whether x10A remained an offset-style field or received a semantic name later.
Verify final accepted prototype after consolidation.

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body claims ninja clean and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0
text_corpus: bot report shows 2 new matches at 100.00%
text_corpus: issuecomment-4056021532 says consolidated into other PRs

No review comments or formal reviewer approvals are present in the loaded slice.

The pointer-math rationale is an author issue comment ending '--Claude'; treat it as PR evidence for the stated rationale, not as proof that no type existed historically.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update owning header prototypes when function bodies are recovered
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2264 recovered fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C bodies and updated gmregclear.h declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures in the same patch.

source_update_candidates.title: Document narrow exception for retained pointer math on unknown void* arguments
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding guidance that raw offset access can remain temporarily when the parameter type is void*, no decompiled callers or supported candidate struct identify the real type, and the offset lies in unnamed padding; require an evidence note and follow-up search terms.
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR contains a concrete rationale for not replacing pointer math with field access, but because it was closed unmerged and consolidated elsewhere, curator review should decide whether this nuance belongs in global standards.

source_update_candidates.title: gmregclear contains game-mode registration-clear helpers with partially recovered local structs
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/gm/gmregclear.c in this PR included recovered bodies for fn_8017DE54 and fn_8017F14C and a local field x10A in lbl_80472D28_t.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmregclear.c function and struct hunks
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact, but PR was closed unmerged and consolidated into other PRs, so final source should be checked before recording.

rejection_notes: Do not record this PR as merged or accepted; loaded metadata has merged_at null and an author closure note says it was consolidated into other PRs.
Do not infer semantic names for fn_8017DE54, fn_8017F14C, x10A, or arg0+0x98 from this slice.
Do not promote the Claude-generated pointer-math explanation into a universal rule without checking the later consolidated PR and current source.
Do not claim objdiff verification beyond the PR body's ninja/fuzzy-match claim and the bot's 100.00% report.
